decision_source: How to set the max_tokens parameter when making a Claude API request for use cases with variable-length responses
stem: In the lab notebook, you configured client.messages.create() with a max_tokens parameter for a single endpoint. Your team now wants to extend the same wrapper to serve both the one-sentence ticket summarizer and a new code-generation route. When you run the code-generation route with the same max_tokens value you used for summarization, Claude's response is cut off mid-function with no API error raised. What does this behavior indicate, and what is the correct fix?

options.A: The cutoff indicates Claude encountered a context window overflow; the fix is to shorten the system prompt to free up output capacity.
options.B: The cutoff indicates max_tokens was reached — a hard stop that Claude does not report as an error; the fix is to set a higher max_tokens value calibrated to the maximum expected output length of the code-generation endpoint specifically.
options.C: The cutoff indicates a transient network timeout during streaming; the fix is to add a retry loop around client.messages.create() so incomplete responses are automatically re-requested.
options.D: The cutoff indicates the model selected is too small for code generation; the fix is to switch to a larger Claude model, which has a higher default output limit.

decision_source: How to structure the messages array in a multi-step agentic workflow where Claude must reference earlier reasoning before acting
stem: In the lab, you built multi-turn conversations by appending each assistant response to the messages list before constructing the next API call with client.messages.create(). Your team is now extending this pattern into a three-step agentic pipeline for a financial audit tool: step one has Claude assess transaction anomalies, step two has it select an investigative action, and step three has it draft a compliance report. A colleague proposes that after step one, you extract only the anomaly score and category fields from Claude's response and pass those as a new user message — discarding the rest of the assistant turn — to keep the context lean. Under what condition does this approach introduce audit risk that the full-history pattern avoids?

options.A: When the anomaly score and category fields are insufficient to reconstruct Claude's full reasoning, step three may produce a compliance report whose conclusions cannot be traced back to the logic Claude applied in step one, creating an incomplete audit trail.
options.B: When the messages list grows beyond 10 turns, client.messages.create() will truncate earlier entries automatically, making the full-history pattern equally unreliable for long pipelines.
options.C: When step two requires Claude to select an action, injecting only structured fields is equivalent to maintaining the full history because Claude re-derives the same reasoning from the same input data.
options.D: When the model parameter is set to claude-sonnet-4-0, assistant messages are cached server-side, so discarding them locally has no effect on the reasoning available to subsequent calls.
